_meta.description: Public/private queues, queue items, routing rules, and work distribution.

mcq.q: In Dynamics 365 Customer Service, what is a Queue used for?
options: Storing escalated cases that have breached an SLA
Holding records that need to be worked on, allowing agents to pick items and organise work
mcq.explanation: Queues are holding areas for work items (cases, activities) waiting to be processed. Agents pick queue items from their assigned queues, ensuring organised work distribution.
wrongExplanations: SLA-breached cases can be routed to queues, but queues themselves are not specifically for SLA violations.
Resolved cases remain as Case records — queues are for active work items.
Agent groups for reporting are typically Teams or Business Units — not queues.

mcq.q: What differentiates a Public Queue from a Private Queue in Dynamics 365?
options: Public queues are visible to all agents; private queues are only visible to agents explicitly added as members
Public queues support SLA tracking; private queues do not
Public queues are for cases; private queues are for activities only
Public queues are managed by administrators; private queues are self-managed by agents
mcq.explanation: Public queues are visible to all agents in the system. Private queues restrict visibility to explicitly added members only, allowing more controlled work distribution.
wrongExplanations: SLA tracking is based on SLA configuration and applies to cases regardless of queue type.
Both public and private queues can hold cases and activities.
Queue management permissions are determined by security roles, not by public or private queue type.

mcq.q: Which record is automatically created and tracks an agent's ownership of a specific queue item?
mcq.explanation: A Queue Item is created when a case or activity is added to a queue. It tracks which agent has picked (is working) the item. When an agent picks an item, the Queue Item's Worked By field is set to that agent.
wrongExplanations: Case Assignment is not a standard entity in Dynamics 365 Customer Service.
Work Streams are Unified Routing configurations that define how work is classified and routed — not individual work ownership records.
Bookings are used in Field Service for scheduling resources — not for queue ownership in Customer Service.

mcq.q: A company wants cases to be automatically assigned to the correct queue based on the case type. Which feature should be configured?
mcq.explanation: Routing Rule Sets evaluate case attributes and automatically route cases to the appropriate queue. Each rule item defines conditions (e.g. Case Type = Question) and the target queue.
wrongExplanations: SLAs define time targets — they do not route cases to queues.
Record Creation Rules create cases from inbound activities — they do not route existing cases to queues.
Entitlement Channels define which contact channels count against a support entitlement — not routing logic.

dragdrop.instruction: Classify each Queue characteristic as belonging to a Public Queue, Private Queue, or both.
items: Visible to all agents in the system
Restricted to explicitly added members only
Queue Item Worked By field tracks ownership
dragdrop.explanation: Public queues are open to all agents and suit general triage. Private queues restrict access to members and suit specialist teams. Queue Item ownership tracking applies to both.
